meta_title: For Students: user onboarding to Solve can't debug code (Intermediate Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to user onboarding and overcome can't debug code. A Intermediate-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Master User Onboarding to Debug Code Like a Pro
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with debugging. Learn how to architect robust user onboarding to isolate logic errors and build production-ready AI apps.
content.answer_block: Debugging isn't just about syntax; it's about state management. By implementing structured user onboarding, you force your application to handle data flow sequentially. This visibility allows you to pinpoint where state transitions fail, effectively turning your onboarding process into a diagnostic tool that reveals hidden logic bugs.
content.intro:
The screen glows, the cursor blinks, and your console is a graveyard of cryptic errors. You’ve reached that inevitable wall: the logic is sound in your head, but the code refuses to cooperate. It’s the classic 'can't debug code' syndrome—a rite of passage for every builder, yet one that stops most students in their tracks. You aren't failing; you're just missing the architectural framework to see your own logic clearly.

Great engineering isn't about writing bug-free code; it's about building systems that make bugs impossible to hide. By integrating a sophisticated user onboarding flow, you transform your application into a modular environment where every interaction is tracked. This is the difference between a prototype that breaks and a product that scales. Let’s refactor your approach and get your project back on track.

steps.title: Architecting State-Driven Onboarding
steps.description: Before fixing bugs, define the onboarding states. This ensures that every user action is predictable and traceable.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a state machine schema for a 3-step user onboarding flow that includes data validation at each transition.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'generate state machine schema for onboarding'

steps.title: Instrumenting Diagnostic Logging
steps.description: Inject logs at every step of the onboarding process to catch silent failures that don't trigger crashes.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Add structured logging to my onboarding functions that capture user input and internal state before/after validation.

steps.title: Implementing Interactive Error Boundaries
steps.description: Wrap your onboarding components in error boundaries so one failure doesn't crash the entire user experience.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a React error boundary component specifically for my onboarding wizard steps.

steps.title: Automating Test Suites for Flows
steps.description: Write integration tests that simulate a user completing the onboarding flow to catch regressions early.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a Playwright test script that simulates a user journey through the onboarding process including error handling.

steps.title: Refactoring via AI-Assisted Review
steps.description: Use Claude Code to perform a final audit of your onboarding logic against your logs to find the root cause of your bugs.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my onboarding codebase and identify any race conditions or state synchronization issues.

pro_tips: Treat onboarding as a database transaction: if a step fails, roll back the user state to the last known good configuration.
Use unique correlation IDs for every onboarding session to trace errors across multiple microservices or API calls.
Implement 'Feature Flags' for onboarding steps to isolate new code paths without risking the entire user journey.
Always log the 'previous state' alongside the 'current state' during onboarding transitions to identify logic drift.
Use 'Shadow Deployments' for onboarding updates to compare how different versions handle edge-case input errors.
content.next_steps: Once your onboarding flow is stable and debugged, move to implementing user analytics to track where users drop off during the flow.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical roadmap to stop chasing errors and start building resilient, high-quality software. The difference between a frustrated student and a hired engineer is the ability to systematically solve complex problems. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
